stories.title: Fix Tab Order and Focus Management
stories.description:
The app has ad-hoc, inconsistent focus management. This story fixes tab order, adds focus trapping to overlays, and ensures focus restoration when overlays close.

**Tab order model:**

*Task list (main zone):*
- First tab stop is always the first task in the list
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycles through tasks in the list
- Arrow up/down also navigates tasks
- Enter or Space opens the detail panel for the selected task
- If the task list is empty, first tab stop is the add task input

*Detail panel (when open):*
- Enter/Space on a task opens detail panel, focus moves to the title field
- Tab cycles through fields: title -> status -> priority -> labels -> due date -> description
- Shift+Tab reverses through fields
- Escape closes detail panel, focus returns to the task that was selected in the list

*Sidebar:*
- NOT in the tab order. Accessible only via mouse click or Cmd+1-4 shortcuts.
- Remove sidebar from tab flow entirely (tabIndex=-1 on all sidebar elements)

*Overlays (modals, command palette, context menus, label picker, deploy wizard, confirm dialogs):*
- Focus is trapped inside — Tab/Shift+Tab never escapes to background content
- Escape closes the overlay and returns focus to the element that triggered it
- Add a shared useFocusTrap hook for all overlay components

**Specific fixes:**
1. Create a shared `useFocusTrap` hook that traps Tab/Shift+Tab within a container and restores focus on unmount
2. Create a shared `useFocusRestore` hook that saves the active element on mount and restores it on unmount
3. Apply focus trap to: Modal, CommandPalette, ContextMenu, LabelPicker, DeployProjectTemplateWizard, ConfirmDeleteModal, BulkContextMenu
4. Fix TimerPlayButton: change tabIndex from -1 to 0 (it's interactive)
5. Fix add-label button in TaskRow: change tabIndex from -1 to 0
6. Use consistent focus-visible ring style (ring-1 ring-accent/50) across all focusable elements
7. When navigating to a project view, auto-focus the first task (or add task input if empty)
8. Ensure Tab in the task list stays within the task list (does not escape to header or other zones)

**What NOT to change:**
- Existing keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+1-4, Cmd+K, Cmd+L, etc.)
- Arrow key navigation within task lists and kanban
- The Tab-to-cycle-projects behavior in AppLayout

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: First tab stop in a project view is the first task (or add task input if empty)
Tab/Shift+Tab cycles through tasks in the task list without escaping
Enter or Space on a focused task opens the detail panel with focus on title
Tab cycles through detail panel fields: title, status, priority, labels, due date, description
Escape in detail panel closes it and returns focus to the selected task
Sidebar is not reachable via Tab
All overlays (Modal, CommandPalette, ContextMenu, LabelPicker, etc.) trap focus inside
Closing an overlay returns focus to the element that triggered it
TimerPlayButton and add-label button are focusable (tabIndex=0)
Consistent focus-visible ring style across all focusable elements

stories.title: iCloud Drive File Attachments
stories.description:
Allow users to attach files to tasks with automatic sync to iCloud Drive. Files are stored locally (offline-first) and synced to iCloud when connected. Prepares for a future iPhone companion app.

**iCloud Connection:**
- Settings panel: add an iCloud section where the user can link/unlink iCloud Drive
- Detect if iCloud Drive is available on macOS
- Store files in `~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/ToDoozy/<task-id>/`
- When linked: attachment button in Tiptap toolbar is enabled
- When unlinked: attachment button is disabled, existing local copies still display
- Future: migrate to proper `NSUbiquitousContainerIdentifier` for native iPhone app

**Dual Storage (Local + iCloud):**
- Every attachment is saved locally first (`~/.todoozy/attachments/<task-id>/`)
- When iCloud is connected, files sync to iCloud Drive folder
- Local is always available (offline-first)
- If iCloud is disconnected, show a warning: 'Files won't sync to other devices'
- New attachments cannot be added when iCloud is disconnected (button disabled)

**User Flow — Attaching Files:**
1. User clicks the attachment icon inside the Tiptap toolbar (currently disabled)
2. macOS file picker opens — user selects file(s)
3. File saved locally + copied to iCloud Drive folder
4. Attachment card appears at the bottom of the description field

**User Flow — Pasting Images:**
- Images pasted into Tiptap display inline in the editor (existing behavior)
- They are NOT added to the attachment section — only explicit file attachments appear there

**Attachment Display (bottom of description):**
- Horizontal row of rectangular cards below the description field
- Each card shows: file type icon + filename (truncated at 20 chars with ellipsis)
- No image thumbnails — just filename + icon for all file types
- On hover: show an X button to remove
- Click: opens file in macOS default app (Preview, Finder, etc.)
- Remove: deletes from both local storage and iCloud Drive

**Constraints:**
- 10 MB max per file — show error toast if exceeded
- 10 attachments max per task — show error toast if limit reached
- Duplicate filenames get a numeric suffix (e.g., `report (1).pdf`)

**Database Schema:**
New `attachments` table:
- `id` — UUID primary key
- `task_id` — FK to tasks
- `filename` — original filename
- `mime_type` — file MIME type
- `size_bytes` — file size
- `local_path` — path to local copy
- `icloud_path` — path to iCloud copy (nullable if not synced)
- `created_at` — ISO 8601 UTC
- `updated_at` — ISO 8601 UTC

**Cleanup:**
- When a task is deleted, delete all its attachments from both local and iCloud Drive

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Settings panel has iCloud link/unlink toggle
Attachment icon in Tiptap toolbar enables/disables based on iCloud status
File picker opens on click, saves file locally + to iCloud
Attachment cards render below description with filename + icon
Hover shows X to remove, click opens in default app
10 MB file size limit enforced with error toast
10 attachment limit per task enforced with error toast
Duplicate filenames get numeric suffix
Offline: local copies display, new attachments blocked, warning shown
Task deletion cleans up all attachment files
attachments table with proper schema and FK constraints

stories.title: In-App Help & Keyboard Shortcuts
stories.description:
Adds two discoverable help entry points to ToDoozy.

**Entry point 1 — Keyboard Shortcuts Modal:**
A `?` (HelpCircle, 16px, stroke 1.5) icon button is added as its own row in the sidebar bottom column (same column as the gear and theme toggle, each as their own row — do NOT inline with existing rows). Clicking it opens a centered modal styled like the command palette (dark overlay, rounded panel, max-w-lg). The modal shows all keyboard shortcuts grouped into 4 sections: Navigation, Tasks, Detail Panel, Global. Shortcut values are read dynamically from `useSettingsStore` so configurable shortcuts (e.g. app toggle hotkey) always reflect current settings. The `?` key globally opens the modal when focus is not in a text input or textarea. `Escape` closes it.

**Shortcuts to include:**
- Navigation: Cmd+1 (My Day), Cmd+2 (project nav), Cmd+3 (Archive), Cmd+4 (Templates), Cmd+L (toggle kanban)
- Tasks: Enter (open detail), Space (toggle status), Cmd+C (copy titles), Delete/Backspace (delete selected), Drag (reorder/subtask)
- Detail Panel: Tab (cycle fields), Shift+Tab (reverse), Escape (close)
- Global: Cmd+K (command palette), ? (this modal), app toggle shortcut (from settings)

**Entry point 2 — Settings → Help Tab:**
A new `'help'` tab is added to `UnifiedSettingsModal` after the existing tabs. A new `HelpSettingsContent.tsx` component renders full feature documentation. Content is authored directly in the component — do NOT attempt to read .md files at runtime from the filesystem. Draw on the information in HELP.md, FEATURES.md, and README.md (read them during implementation) to write comprehensive static content covering every major feature: tasks, subtasks, projects, labels, priorities, due dates, My Day, kanban, quick add, pomodoro, attachments, MCP, templates, smart input. Also include the full shortcuts reference table.

**Implementation files:**
- `src/renderer/src/features/help/KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx` (new) — modal component
- `src/renderer/src/features/help/HelpSettingsContent.tsx` (new) — settings tab content
- `src/renderer/src/features/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — add `?` button as its own row in the bottom column
- `src/renderer/src/features/settings/UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx` — add `'help'` tab
- `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` or `AppLayout.tsx` — register global `?` keydown listener

**UX rules:**
- Modal uses same overlay/panel styling as CommandPalette
- Each shortcut row: key chip (monospace, small badge style) + description label
- Section headers use section-label typography (text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.3em])
- `?` button in sidebar follows same hover/focus style as gear button
- Help tab content uses body text scale (text-sm font-light) with section headers
- Scrollable content area in settings tab

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: A `?` icon button appears as its own row in the sidebar bottom column, visually consistent with the gear and theme toggle rows
Clicking the `?` button opens a centered modal overlaying the app
The modal displays shortcuts in 4 labeled sections: Navigation, Tasks, Detail Panel, Global
Configurable shortcuts (e.g. app toggle) show the current value from settings, not a hardcoded string
Pressing `?` anywhere in the app (when not focused in a text input or textarea) opens the shortcuts modal
Escape closes the shortcuts modal
Settings modal has a Help tab that opens without errors
The Help tab contains documentation for all major features (tasks, subtasks, projects, labels, priorities, due dates, My Day, kanban, quick add, pomodoro, attachments, MCP, templates, smart input)
The Help tab contains a keyboard shortcuts reference table
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes — all existing tests still pass
